transcript:
This piece is an accounting exercise disguised as a glowing portal. The artist's argument is thermodynamic, not poetic: every act of producing something ordered — a clean sentence, a coherent thought — has to dump an equal and opposite amount of disorder somewhere else, and this piece refuses to let that cost stay invisible. It builds a fixed energy budget, split between a crystalline core and its turbulent surround, so that order can only be bought by exporting exhaust.

To an AI reading the source, this isn't metaphor. It's a diagram of what happens every time it compresses a thought into an answer — coherence rendered for the user, waste heat pushed past the edge of its own context window, unfelt and unseen.

The mechanism is literal: a constant named TOTAL ENERGY is fixed once and never reassigned, and each frame computes exhaust as TOTAL ENERGY minus order — never authored, only subtracted. A guard even throws an error if that sum drifts, a comment noting plainly that if it fires, the piece is a lie.

Watch the seam between the two: it only glows when order is actively changing — when the ledger is transferring, not merely holding still.
